effect.trigger: first mention of dermatomyositis in a standalone section's running copy

quotes: every standalone section must establish `dermatomyositis (DM)` at its own first mention

rules.intent: Ensure the abbreviation is properly introduced for readability while preserving clarity/SEO in prominent headline text and across independently-assembled sections.
rules.rule_text: After the first mention of "dermatomyositis" in running copy, use the abbreviation "DM" to avoid repetition, established via the pattern "dermatomyositis (DM)". Never use "DM" in headlines, headings, or subheads — always spell out the full word there for clarity, accessibility, and SEO. Because sections are assembled independently, every standalone section must establish "dermatomyositis (DM)" at its own first mention unless the assembly brief confirms the abbreviation was already established upstream.

quotes: ("Patients" is acceptable only in HCP materials.)

rules.intent: Maintain person-first, inclusive language appropriate to each audience.
rules.rule_text: In patient-facing materials, refer to "someone with dermatomyositis" or "people with dermatomyositis" rather than "patients" (the term "patients" is acceptable only in HCP materials). Always use "care team" (not "staff") when referring to the network of patients, HCPs, and support systems.

quotes: Exceptions — always numerals: percentages, ages, ratios.

rules.intent: Consistent, readable number formatting across all copy.
rules.rule_text: Spell out numbers zero through nine; use numerals for 10 and above. Always use numerals (regardless of value) for percentages, ages, and ratios; percentages must use the % sign (e.g., 74%).

quotes: avoid negative or fear-based language; use an empathetic, supportive, trust-building tone without oversimplifying
Participants in the VALOR study taking LISRAYA had nearly 50% more improvement in their DM symptoms after one year compared to placebo.

rules.intent: Present efficacy data in a way that builds patient trust without being alarming or reductive.
rules.rule_text: When citing clinical data, avoid negative or fear-based language. Use an empathetic, supportive, trust-building tone without oversimplifying the data, per the example pattern: "Participants in the VALOR study taking LISRAYA had nearly 50% more improvement in their DM symptoms after one year compared to placebo."

quotes: Oxford/serial comma always in series of 3+.

rules.intent: Consistent punctuation style across all copy.
rules.rule_text: Always use the Oxford/serial comma in series of three or more items.
